articles.title: Why India's first hydrogen train is special - explained
articles.summary: With the launch of a hydrogen train, India has undoubtedly taken a big leap in the sphere of greener transportation. India’s first hydrogen-powered train has 10 coaches, with the capacity to carry 2,600 passengers. It will run between Jind and Sonipat in Haryana.

articles.title: India’s first hydrogen train: The 89 km journey that could change rail travel forever
articles.summary: India’s first hydrogen train begins a new era of green rail travel. Running from Jind to Sonipat, the zero-emission train brings powerful technology and cleaner mobility.

articles.title: 'India not just building airports, but...': Israeli envoy after Guwahati, Navi Mumbai airports get global recognition
articles.summary: "Hope to see Indian companies building an airport in Israel as well soon!” The envoy’s remark signals growing interest in international collaboration as India’s aviation infrastructure receives global attention.

articles.title: Not just a train tunnel: How Switzerland’s mega project is quietly protecting the Alps
articles.summary: Deep beneath the Alps, Switzerland is proving that the biggest answer to a climate challenge can sometimes be hidden underground.

articles.title: Gold price prediction: Is it time to buy gold on dips? Check July 17, 2026 outlook
articles.summary: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are favouring a buy on dips strategy, says Jateen Trivedi, VP Research Analyst - Commodity and Currency, LKP Securities.

articles.title: Asian markets tumble as AI stock sell-off deepens; Nikkei sinks nearly 6%, HSI plunges 2%
articles.summary: Asian stock markets experienced significant selling pressure on Friday. Japan's Nikkei 225 and Taiwan's benchmark saw sharp declines. Concerns over artificial intelligence stock valuations and Middle East conflict weighed on investors.

articles.title: Stock market rally: Sensex rises over 750 points, Nifty50 crosses 24,250 - top reasons for rise
articles.summary: The sharp rally in Indian equities came even as global markets remained under heavy pressure. Japan's Nikkei tumbled more than 5%, while Taiwan's Weighted Index plunged 6% as the sell-off in semiconductor stocks continued.

articles.title: India's first hydrogen-powered train flagged off by PM Modi; check route, timings, how it works
articles.summary: India’s first hydrogen train is a 10-coach passenger offering. According to Indian Railways, globally the few operational or under testing hydrogen trains have 2-3 passenger coaches, making India’s first hydrogen train the longest of its type in the world.

articles.title: Tata Sons stake: Shapoorji Pallonji looking at various options to cut its high debt
articles.summary: Tata Sons stake: A share swap in Tata Group listed companies is being spoken of; it is critical for the company to decide quickly.

articles.title: Vistara overbooking row: Airline told to pay ₹1 lakh for denying boarding to judge with confirmed ticket
articles.summary: The case relates to Bhupendra Kumar Vasnikar, an Additional District Judge in Chhattisgarh, who was returning with his family from a vacation in Kashmir in May 2023

articles.title: Rupee rises 14 paise in early trade as Dalal Street cheers; oil adds limited gains
articles.summary: The rupee opened stronger against the US dollar on Friday, gaining fourteen paise in early trade. Upbeat equity markets provided support for the domestic currency's early gains. However, sustained foreign fund outflows and firm crude oil prices capped further appreciation.

articles.title: Hydrogen train, Rs 25K cr in projects: PM Modi's agenda in Haryana, Chandigarh & Punjab
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will launch development projects exceeding twenty-four thousand eight hundred crore rupees. These initiatives span highways, railways, healthcare, and education infrastructure across three regions.

articles.title: After 400 years underwater, this priceless treasure has finally been found—and more may still be waiting
articles.summary: The silver bar was found about 50 feet below the surface after divers aboard the salvage vessel DARE detected what was described as a "very strong target" using a metal detector

articles.title: 'I was rejected by nearly 100 banks': Razorpay's Harshil Mathur on building $7.5 bn fintech giant
articles.summary: Unlike traditional payment companies that focused on banks and large enterprises, Razorpay was designed with startups and merchants in mind

articles.title: Where are gold and silver prices heading? Inflation, oil and Fed bets hold the key
A two-month internship worth ₹67 lakh? Viral IIT job post has the internet asking if the offer is too good to be true
articles.summary: The viral listing, which requires a minimum CGPA of 8.5, is widely believed to be from quant trading firm Quadeye. While the company has not confirmed the ₹67 lakh internship figure, its careers page lists a minimum ₹93 LPA full-time package upon conversion.

articles.title: 'The biggest benefit isn't the salary': Indian engineer explains why he won't return to an Indian workplace
articles.summary: What stands out most for Singh, however, is the company's approach to work-life balance.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Warren Buffett: ‘I always knew i was going to be rich...’
articles.summary: Known as the "Oracle of Omaha," Warren Buffett is one of the most successful investors of all time. Buffett chairs Berkshire Hathaway.

articles.title: Crude heads for biggest weekly gain in months amid intensifying Middle East crisis
articles.summary: Oil prices surged as escalating US-Iran military tensions raised supply disruption concerns. Brent crude traded at $85.34 and WTI crude at $80.08 per barrel. Recent US strikes aimed to degrade Iranian military capabilities, while Iran retaliated against regional allies.

articles.title: OTT releases this week (July 13 - July 19): 'Maa Inti Bangaaram', 'Chand Mera Dil', 'Heartstopper Forever' and more to watch this weekend
articles.summary: Alongside these are several international originals and Indian shows arriving on Netflix, JioHotstar, Prime Video, Apple TV+ and MX Player

articles.title: Uber to buy Delivery Hero in $15 billion deal
articles.summary: Uber will buy Germany's Delivery Hero for $14.8 billion. This creates the largest food delivery group outside China. The acquisition helps Uber compete with rivals like Just Eat. Uber expects the transaction to finish in the second half of next year.

articles.title: Fuel-saving tech may be rewarded
articles.summary: New fuel efficiency rules may reward various fuel-saving technologies. Manufacturers can earn compliance benefits for technologies like start-stop systems. These approved technologies will offer credits equivalent to grams of CO2 per kilometer.

articles.title: MG eyes up to 80% sales from 'new energy' vehicles
articles.summary: JSW MG Motor India is increasing its focus on new-energy vehicles this year. Higher fuel costs and awareness of savings are driving EV adoption significantly. The company expects new-energy vehicles to comprise eighty percent of its total sales.

articles.title: Govt to take steps to boost liquidity for small biz
articles.summary: The government plans new tools to boost liquidity for micro, small, and medium enterprises. Amendments to the MSME Development Act will be moved in Parliament soon. These changes aim to remove business hurdles and improve cash flow for these crucial companies.

articles.title: Banks can't sell back seized assets to defaulters: RBI
articles.summary: RBI has barred defaulting borrowers from repurchasing seized properties. New directions effective October 1, 2026, tighten norms for stressed accounts. Banks must adopt board-approved policies for asset recognition and recovery efforts.

articles.title: Pricing, not volume, drives India Inc in Q1; revenue estimated to grow 11-11.5% despite supply disruptions: Crisil
Govt to take equity stakes in semiconductor startups
articles.summary: Works on a model of larger, milestone-linked funding

articles.title: India’s First Hydrogen-Powered Train Flagged Off by PM Modi: 10 Facts & Photos You Shouldn’t Miss
articles.summary: ​India’s first hydrogen-powered train has been launched! Marking a major milestone for Indian Railways, the country’s first hydrogen was flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on July 17.

articles.title: BT Explainer | When banks can seize property from loan defaulters: RBI's new rules decoded
articles.summary: RBI has introduced a comprehensive framework governing how banks should acquire, value and dispose of properties taken over from defaulting borrowers.

articles.title: ‘Aim to make India the world's most authentic experience destination:’ Shekhawat
In tough Q1, ITC Hotels sees profit, revenue zoom
Wipro Q1 trails peers; revenue down 1.4% to $2.6 bn
Jio Financial's payments business turns around as digital payment value jumps 2.5x, deposits rise 72%
articles.summary: Jio Payment Solutions, the company's merchant acquiring and payment processing arm, recorded a Total Payment Value (TPV) of ₹19,208 crore during the June quarter, a 2.5-fold increase year-on-year.

articles.title: Thinking of switching to electric? These 5 scooters make the decision straightforward
articles.summary: From Ather 450X to Hero Vida V1, here are India's top 5 electric scooters offering 126–165 km range, fast charging and smart features — the smartest two-wheeler buys of 2026.

articles.title: Ex-Paisabazaar executive Dhiraj Kalra joins Fundamento as AI startup expands in India, US
articles.summary: The company recently launched its platform for lenders in the US and says it now manages over 10 million customer interactions every month across banking, lending and insurance.

articles.title: No gas connection! Instamart to deliver HPCL cylinders — but can quick commerce crack a regulated market?
articles.summary: HPCL's quick-commerce pilot promises greater convenience, but also puts the spotlight on customer verification, delivery safeguards and how a regulated commodity will be sold online.

articles.title: Raghuram Rajan picked for US Fed review panel: Here's the role he'll play
articles.summary: Raghuram Rajan will serve on the Balance Sheet Policy task force alongside Harvard economist Karen Dynan and former Federal Reserve Governor Jeremy Stein.

articles.title: A man zoomed into Google Maps—and may have uncovered a 390-million-year-old secret hidden on Earth
articles.summary: From a casual Google Maps click to an international science stage, a nearly perfect ring spotted on Google Maps in a remote part of Quebec, Canada, has become the focus of scientific research.

articles.title: India's social protection coverage crosses 100 crore in 2026, says Labour Minister; ILO recognises milestone
articles.summary: The ILO has recognised India's social protection system as reaching 1 billion people in 2026. The acknowledgement, highlighted by Mansukh Mandaviya at the BRICS meeting, underscores the scale of the country's welfare expansion.

articles.title: Not just pets: These 10 dogs cost more than some luxury cars
articles.summary: From the ₹14.45 crore Tibetan Mastiff to luxury companion breeds, explore the world’s most expensive dogs and discover why rarity, beauty and pedigree drive their jaw-dropping prices.
